solutions.title: Automotive Infotainment Storage Solution
solutions.subtitle: AEC-Q100 qualified embedded storage for next-generation automotive infotainment systems
solutions.description: Comprehensive automotive-grade storage solution using Longsys AEC-Q100 qualified eMMC and UFS products for infotainment, ADAS, and telematics applications
solutions.longDescription:
Longsys Automotive Infotainment Storage Solution provides AEC-Q100 qualified embedded storage for next-generation automotive infotainment, digital instrument clusters, and ADAS applications. This solution leverages Longsys automotive-grade eMMC and UFS products to deliver reliable, high-performance storage in harsh automotive environments.

The solution addresses critical automotive requirements including wide temperature operation (-40°C to +105°C), high vibration resistance, power-loss protection, and long-term data retention. With interface options from cost-effective eMMC 5.1 to high-performance UFS 3.1, the solution scales from mainstream infotainment systems to premium digital cockpits.

Key technical features include AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification, advanced error correction, static and dynamic wear leveling, and power-loss protection to ensure data integrity during unexpected power events. The UFS 3.1 interface delivers up to 2100MB/s sequential read speed for fast boot times and smooth multimedia playback.

LiTong provides comprehensive automotive storage support including AEC-Q100 documentation, PPAP support, schematic review, and thermal design guidance. Our automotive FAE team has extensive experience with OEM qualification requirements and can assist with design optimization for specific automotive applications.

The solution has been successfully deployed in multiple Tier-1 automotive projects, demonstrating reliable operation in harsh automotive environments with zero field failures over 2+ years of production.

faeInsights.title: Principal FAE - Automotive Storage
faeInsights.content: This automotive infotainment storage solution has been successfully deployed in multiple Tier-1 automotive projects. The key to success is selecting the right storage interface based on performance requirements and cost targets. For premium systems with rich multimedia and navigation, UFS 3.1 provides the best user experience. For mainstream systems, eMMC 5.1 offers excellent value while meeting all automotive reliability requirements. I always recommend implementing proper power sequencing and brown-out detection to maximize storage reliability. The AEC-Q100 qualification process is rigorous, and Longsys automotive products have demonstrated excellent field reliability in harsh automotive environments.
faeInsights.highlight: Proven solution deployed in multiple Tier-1 automotive infotainment systems
faeInsights.insightLogic: Having supported multiple Tier-1 automotive projects over the past 5 years, I've learned that automotive storage design requires careful attention to three critical areas: power sequencing, thermal management, and EMC compliance. The most common issue I see is improper power sequencing - VCC must be stable before VCCQ, and brown-out conditions must be detected and handled gracefully. For thermal design, automotive under-hood applications can reach 105°C ambient, so proper heat sinking is essential. EMC is often underestimated - automotive OEMs have strict CISPR 25 requirements that require careful PCB layout with continuous ground planes. The key to success is engaging with our automotive FAE team early in the design phase to review schematics and layout before PCB fabrication.

faqs.question: What is the difference between AEC-Q100 Grade 2 and Grade 3?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q100 Grade 2 requires operation from -40°C to +105°C ambient temperature, suitable for automotive interior and some under-hood applications. Grade 3 requires -40°C to +85°C, suitable for passenger compartment applications. Grade 2 has more stringent testing requirements and is preferred for applications with higher temperature exposure. Both grades ensure automotive reliability but Grade 2 provides wider operating range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select Grade 2 for under-hood or high-temperature locations, Grade 3 for passenger compartment.

faqs.question: How do I handle power sequencing for automotive storage?
faqs.answer: Proper power sequencing is critical for reliable operation. eMMC/UFS VCC should be stable before VCCQ (I/O voltage). Implement brown-out detection to prevent operation during low voltage conditions. Use sufficient decoupling capacitors (typically 2.2uF + 0.1uF) close to power pins. Include power-loss detection circuit to enable graceful shutdown. Follow the reference design power sequencing timing requirements provided in the integration guide.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow reference design power sequencing for reliable automotive operation.

faqs.question: What filesystem is recommended for automotive storage?
faqs.answer: For automotive applications, use filesystems with power-loss tolerance. ext4 with journal checksums is commonly used in Linux-based automotive systems. For raw NAND access without FTL, use UBIFS or JFFS2. Avoid FAT32 for critical applications due to corruption risk during power events. Implement read-only root filesystem with separate read-write partitions for data logging. Consider using overlay filesystems for system updates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ext4 with journal checksums or UBIFS for automotive Linux systems.

faqs.question: How do I calculate storage endurance for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Calculate endurance using: (NAND P/E cycles × Capacity × Over-provisioning factor) / Daily write volume / 365 days. For automotive infotainment with mostly read operations, typical lifespan exceeds 10 years. For data logging applications, calculate based on log frequency and size. Longsys provides endurance calculation tools and can assist with application-specific analysis. Consider using SLC mode or larger capacity for high-write applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong FAEs for application-specific endurance calculations.

faqs.question: What EMI considerations are important for automotive storage?
faqs.answer: Automotive storage must meet CISPR 25 EMC requirements. Use proper PCB layout with continuous ground plane under storage device. Keep trace lengths short and matched for high-speed signals. Implement proper decoupling and filtering. Shield critical signals if necessary. The BGA package provides good EMC performance due to short bond wires. Follow automotive OEM EMC requirements which may be more stringent than standard CISPR 25.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow automotive EMC design guidelines and OEM requirements for storage layout.

solutions.title: Industrial Edge Computing Storage Solution
solutions.subtitle: High-reliability storage for industrial automation and edge computing applications
solutions.description: Industrial-grade storage solution using Longsys industrial SSDs and embedded storage for factory automation, robotics, and edge computing systems
solutions.longDescription:
Longsys Industrial Edge Computing Storage Solution provides high-reliability storage for factory automation, robotics, and edge AI applications. This solution leverages Longsys industrial-grade SSDs and embedded storage to deliver consistent performance in harsh industrial environments with 24/7 operation requirements.

The solution addresses critical industrial requirements including wide temperature operation (-40°C to +85°C), high vibration resistance, power-loss protection, and high endurance for continuous write operations. With multiple interface options including SATA III, NVMe PCIe 3.0 x4, eMMC, and UFS, the solution supports diverse industrial applications from data logging to edge AI inference.

Key technical features include industrial temperature range, power-loss protection with onboard capacitors, high endurance NAND (1000+ TBW), 2 million hour MTBF rating, and thermal throttling protection. The NVMe interface delivers up to 3500MB/s sequential read speed for high-throughput edge AI and machine vision applications.

LiTong provides comprehensive industrial storage support including thermal design guidance, power sequencing recommendations, and integration assistance. Our industrial FAE team has extensive experience with Industry 4.0 applications and can assist with solution optimization for specific factory automation requirements.

The solution has been successfully deployed in smart factory equipment, edge AI vision systems, and SCADA data logging applications with demonstrated reliability in demanding industrial environments.

faeInsights.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Storage
faeInsights.content: This industrial edge storage solution addresses the unique challenges of factory automation and edge computing. The key consideration is matching the storage interface to the application performance requirements. For data logging and HMI applications, SATA SSDs provide excellent reliability at lower cost. For edge AI and machine vision requiring high throughput, NVMe SSDs deliver the necessary performance. I always emphasize the importance of proper thermal design - industrial systems often have limited airflow, so thermal throttling protection is essential. The power-loss protection feature has proven critical in industrial environments where power quality can be poor. Longsys industrial products have demonstrated excellent field reliability in demanding factory automation deployments.
faeInsights.highlight: Proven solution for Industry 4.0 applications with excellent field reliability
faeInsights.insightLogic: Supporting industrial edge computing deployments has taught me that reliability in harsh environments requires attention to details that consumer applications can ignore. The number one failure mode I see is thermal throttling in sealed enclosures - industrial systems often have limited airflow and high ambient temperatures. I always recommend thermal modeling early in the design phase and implementing thermal throttling in software. Power quality is another critical factor - industrial environments often have voltage spikes and brownouts that can corrupt data. Power-loss protection is essential, not optional. For interface selection, match the performance to the application - don't over-specify NVMe for data logging when SATA is sufficient. The 2M hour MTBF rating is only achievable with proper thermal design and power quality.

keyTakeaways: Thermal throttling is the primary failure mode in industrial systems
Power-loss protection is essential for data integrity
Match interface performance to application requirements
Implement thermal monitoring and throttling in software

faqs.question: What is the difference between consumer and industrial SSDs?
faqs.answer: Industrial SSDs feature wider temperature ranges (-40°C to +85°C vs 0°C to 70°C), higher endurance (1000+ TBW vs 150-600 TBW), power-loss protection, and enhanced reliability testing. Industrial SSDs use higher-grade NAND and controllers designed for 24/7 operation. They also offer longer product lifecycle support (typically 5+ years) and fixed BOM (Bill of Materials) to ensure consistency. Consumer SSDs are optimized for cost and performance in typical usage scenarios.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select industrial SSDs for harsh environments and mission-critical applications.

faqs.question: How do I implement power-loss protection?
faqs.answer: Power-loss protection uses onboard capacitors to provide emergency power for completing in-flight writes. For systems without integrated PLP, implement external UPS or supercapacitor backup. Ensure sufficient hold-up time (typically 10-20ms) for the SSD to flush its cache. Implement power-fail detection circuit to trigger graceful shutdown. Some industrial SSDs include integrated PLP - verify specifications when selecting. Test power-loss scenarios thoroughly during system validation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select SSDs with integrated PLP or implement external power backup.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for industrial SSDs?
faqs.answer: Industrial SSDs require adequate thermal management to prevent throttling and ensure reliability. Ensure case temperature stays below 70°C under worst-case conditions. Use thermal pads to conduct heat to enclosure. For M.2 SSDs, consider heatsinks or thermal solutions. Monitor SMART temperature attributes for thermal management. Implement thermal throttling in software if needed. In sealed enclosures, ensure sufficient thermal mass or active cooling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for <70°C case temperature with proper thermal conduction paths.

faqs.question: How do I ensure data integrity in industrial applications?
faqs.answer: Implement multiple layers of data protection: Use industrial SSDs with power-loss protection. Implement RAID 1 for critical data. Use filesystems with journaling (ext4, NTFS). Enable SSD SMART monitoring for predictive maintenance. Implement regular backup procedures. Use error detection and correction in application software. Consider using SLC cache mode for write-intensive applications. Test thoroughly under worst-case conditions including power cycling and thermal stress.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement multiple redundancy layers for mission-critical industrial data.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifespan of industrial SSDs?
faqs.answer: Industrial SSD lifespan depends on write workload and capacity. For typical industrial applications with <50GB daily writes, a 1TB industrial SSD (1000+ TBW) provides 50+ years of service life. For heavy write applications, calculate using: (TBW rating / Daily writes) / 365 days. Monitor wear leveling count via SMART to track remaining life. Most industrial applications see less than 10% wear over 5 years. Longsys provides endurance estimation tools for specific applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate lifespan based on TBW rating and actual write workload.

faqs.question: What is the best storage configuration for gaming?
faqs.answer: For optimal gaming performance, use an NVMe SSD (3500MB/s+) for your operating system and frequently played games, and a high-capacity SATA SSD (2TB+) for your game library. This provides fast load times for active games while maintaining cost-effective storage for large libraries.
faqs.decisionGuide: NVMe for OS and active games, SATA for game library storage.

faqs.question: What storage solutions does Longsys offer?
faqs.answer: Longsys offers comprehensive storage solutions including automotive infotainment storage with AEC-Q100 qualified eMMC and UFS products, industrial edge computing storage with wide temperature range SSDs, enterprise data center solutions with high-capacity NVMe SSDs, and consumer storage solutions. Each solution is optimized for specific application requirements with appropriate performance, reliability, and cost characteristics. As an authorized distributor, LiTong provides complete technical support for solution implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong FAEs to discuss your specific storage requirements and solution options.

faqs.question: How do I select the right storage solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right storage solution requires understanding your application requirements including performance needs, operating environment, capacity requirements, and budget constraints. For automotive applications, AEC-Q100 qualified products are required. For industrial applications, wide temperature range and high endurance are critical. For consumer applications, cost and performance balance is important. LiTong FAEs can provide detailed analysis and recommendations based on your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Engage LiTong FAEs early in your design process for optimal solution selection.

faqs.question: Does Longsys provide reference designs for storage solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, Longsys provides comprehensive reference designs for various storage applications including automotive infotainment systems, industrial control systems, and consumer electronics. Reference designs include schematic diagrams, PCB layout recommendations, BOM lists, and software integration guides. These designs have been validated and can significantly accelerate your development process. Contact LiTong for access to reference designs and technical documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request reference designs from LiTong to accelerate your storage solution development.

faqs.question: What technical support does LiTong provide for storage solutions?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive technical support for Longsys storage solutions including product selection guidance, schematic review, PCB layout recommendations, firmware customization, and performance optimization. Our FAE team has deep expertise in storage technologies and can assist with endurance calculations, thermal design, and signal integrity analysis. We also provide reference designs, application notes, and evaluation kits to support your development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Engage LiTong FAEs throughout your design cycle for optimal results.

faqs.question: Can Longsys storage solutions be customized for specific requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, Longsys offers customization options for storage solutions including custom firmware configurations, special capacity requirements, custom form factors, and enhanced endurance options. For high-volume applications, Longsys can develop custom storage solutions tailored to specific requirements. Contact LiTong to discuss your customization needs and volume requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for customization options and high-volume requirements.
